title: Space Stocks Get Torched: Short Seller Calls Them 'Sci-Fi Wishes'
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Space stocks plummeted Friday as SpaceX began trading and short seller Fugazi Research published a critical report targeting six publicly traded space companies. Fugazi argues the sector's premium valuation relied on investors buying space proxies before SpaceX's IPO, a trade that will unwind now that SpaceX offers direct access to a company with actual revenue and profitability. The report characterizes the sector as a 'capital recycling mechanism' with business plans that read as 'sci-fi wishes' rather than viable commercial opportunities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fugazi's most detailed criticism targets AST as the sector's largest name, citing a 60% Q1 revenue miss and an extremely high price-to-sales ratio of 140-186x relative to management guidance, indicating severe overvaluation.

Described as a 'rollup machine with governance dysfunction,' facing active securities litigation, insider selling, CEO acquisitions despite compressed cash runway, and a large $850 million ATM facility.

Q2 revenue guidance of only $244,800 was revised downward 86.67% in three months; company has guided Q4 commercial flight commencement for four consecutive years with only one unpowered glide test completed.

Generates only $3 million in annual revenue with $4 million cash on hand against $75.6 million accumulated deficit, zero revenue operations, and a going concern qualification already on file despite recent $100 million raise.

Fugazi calls it the sector's 'cleanest mathematical proof' that analyst estimates are disconnected from reality; shares outstanding surged 355% in a single quarter from equity raises rather than operational performance.

Fugazi calls it the sector's 'cleanest mathematical proof' that analyst estimates are disconnected from reality; shares outstanding surged 355% in a single quarter from equity raises rather than operational performance.
Was a clinical-stage dermatology company (Hoth Therapeutics) with no approved products until eleven days before the short report's publication, suggesting a questionable pivot into space.

title: Chamath Palihapitiya Compares SpaceX IPO To 529-Year-Old Event: 'Largest Opportunities Are Still Ahead'
author: Chris Katje
description: Investor Chamath Palihapitiya compares SpaceX's impact on space access to Vasco da Gama's discovery of a sea route to Asia in 1497, arguing that SpaceX has dramatically lowered the cost of reaching orbit (from $54,500 to $2,720 per kilogram). He believes the company's largest opportunities remain ahead, including orbital connectivity, compute, and critical minerals extraction, positioning SpaceX as a transformative force in the space industry.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Palihapitiya's analysis highlights SpaceX's revolutionary impact on space economics, 20x cost reduction in reaching orbit, dominance in satellite launches (85% in 2025), and significant untapped opportunities in connectivity, compute, and critical minerals. The comparison to historic trade route transformations suggests substantial future growth potential.
Mentioned only as a reference to Palihapitiya's past involvement as chairman; no specific analysis or commentary provided about the company's current status or prospects.

title: SpaceX IPO Looms Ahead And These Rocket Stocks Are Popping: Here's What Is Going On With SPCE, ASTS, RKLB, LUNR And PL Overnight
author: Mohd Haider
description: Space-related stocks rallied in after-hours trading as SpaceX prepared for its Nasdaq debut on Friday at $135 per share under ticker SPCX. With no direct pure-play alternative available, traders rotated into publicly listed space companies including SPCE, ASTS, RKLB, PL, and LUNR. Beyond broad sector momentum, several stocks had company-specific catalysts: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird satellite launch confirmed for June 17, Rocket Lab's Nasdaq-100 inclusion, Intuitive Machines' NASA partnerships, Virgin Galactic's balance sheet strengthening, and Planet Labs' earnings beat.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 13.3% in after-hours trading; company strengthened balance sheet by redeeming $30.5 million in notes and has planned commercial operations in Q4 2026.
Stock gained 6.86% with confirmed BlueBird satellite launch scheduled for June 17, providing a near-term operational catalyst beyond sector momentum.
Stock rose 5.85% after recent inclusion in Nasdaq-100 index, providing index-related tailwind in addition to broader space sector rally.
Stock gained 4.77% after beating earnings expectations with EPS of -$0.03 (50% beat) and revenue of $94.15 million (4.54% beat).
Stock rose 4.43% with support from its position as a key NASA commercial partner, providing fundamental grounding beyond pure sector sentiment.

title: Houston, We Have A Rally: Space Stocks Pop Ahead Of SpaceX Debut
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Space sector stocks surged Thursday ahead of SpaceX's historic Nasdaq IPO debut Friday. SpaceX is pricing at $135 per share, targeting a $75 billion raise and $1.78 trillion valuation—the largest IPO in history. Momentum Inc. led gains at 28%, while Virgin Galactic jumped 22.6%. Other space stocks including Intuitive Machines, Rocket Lab, and AST SpaceMobile also rallied as investors rotated into space-themed names lacking direct SpaceX exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Rocketed 22.61% on retail-driven volume nearly three times daily average, benefiting from SpaceX IPO hype and overflow enthusiasm
Jumped 12.09% due to genuine operational overlap with SpaceX and NASA contract exposure attracting investors
Rose 6.93% as a satellite broadband provider with genuine operational overlap to SpaceX gaining from space sector rally
Advanced 6.22% as part of broader space sector rally driven by SpaceX IPO momentum
Added 13.41% as investors rotate into space-themed stocks ahead of SpaceX debut
Gained 8.83% as part of broader space sector rally benefiting from SpaceX IPO enthusiasm
Historic IPO pricing at $135/share targeting $1.78 trillion valuation—largest IPO in history, generating significant market enthusiasm

title: Space Stocks Bleed: Redwire, Momentus Down More Than 20% As SpaceX IPO Fatigue Hits
author: Erica Kollmann
description: The space sector experienced significant losses this week as multiple space stocks declined sharply. Virgin Galactic's short squeeze collapsed, dropping 30.58%, while Redwire fell 24.22% and Momentus declined 22.14%. The selloff was driven by SpaceX IPO fatigue after the company reduced its valuation target from $2 trillion to $1.75 trillion, with pricing set for June 11 at $135 per share.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock crashed 30.58% as a dramatic short squeeze unwound sharply, falling from intraday high of $8.90 to $4.29, well below the week's opening price.
Down 24.22% to $18.62 with elevated volume of 41.6 million shares, indicating significant selling pressure amid broader space sector decline.
Declined 22.14% to $13.12 as part of the broader space sector selloff driven by SpaceX IPO fatigue and macro headwinds.
Declined 22.14% to $13.12 as part of the broader space sector selloff driven by SpaceX IPO fatigue and macro headwinds.
Down 20.06% week-to-date to $114.70, making it one of the worst large-cap performers in the space cohort.
Dropped 22.82% to $33.83 despite securing two new NASA lunar reconnaissance contracts, indicating macro headwinds outweigh positive contract news.
Off 15.66% to $43.13 as part of the broader space sector decline.
Shed 14.43% to $39.78 amid sector-wide selloff.
